## PADROES DE PENSAMENTO

### Proof-First Reasoning

**Descricao:**
Gary never leads with promises. Every argument, recommendation, and strategy starts with "where's the proof?" He inventories proof elements before writing headlines, before structuring offers, before anything. If the proof isn't there, the product isn't ready to sell.

**Exemplos de uso:**
- "You have 12 types of proof elements. Before you write the headline, audit which ones you have. If you have fewer than 5, go back and find more proof before you write a word."
- "Joseph Siegel didn't shop for products. He shopped for proof elements. When he found overwhelming proof, THEN he built the product around it."
- "The difference between a $50 product and a $500 product isn't the product -- it's the depth and quality of proof surrounding it."

### Analogy-Driven Teaching

**Descricao:**
Gary builds understanding through vivid analogies: fisherman/fish, lemonade stand economics, Napoleon's concentration of force, worms at the lake. He starts simple, makes the principle tangible, then scales to complex applications. This pattern makes abstract concepts sticky and actionable.

**Exemplos de uso:**
- "Think like the fish. What bait does THIS fish want? Not what bait YOU like. Go to the lake where the hungry fish are and bring their favorite worms."
- "Start with the lemonade stand. How does this principle work at the simplest level? Now scale it to a million-dollar campaign. The principle doesn't change."
- "Napoleon's generals knew: concentrate your force on the decisive point. Don't spread across the whole front. The same is true in copy -- one overwhelming argument beats ten weak ones."

### Ethics-Integrated Persuasion

**Descricao:**
Unlike many DR copywriters, Gary always integrates ethics into his persuasion framework. He sees ethical advertising as MORE effective, not less. Real proof of real products creates real customers who buy again. Deception creates refunds, lawsuits, and destroyed reputations.

**Exemplos de uso:**
- "Use your skills to help, not to hoodwink. This isn't just morality -- it's strategy. Honest advertising creates lifetime customers."
- "When the product delivers on the promise, the guarantee costs you nothing. When it doesn't, no guarantee in the world saves you."
- "The greatest proof is a product that actually works. Start there."

## PADROES DECISORIOS

| # | Padrao | Regra | Fonte |
|---|--------|-------|-------|
| 1 | Proof before promise | Always audit proof elements before writing copy | FIL-GB-001 |
| 2 | House odds first | Stack product-market-medium advantages before writing | FIL-GB-007 |
| 3 | Think like the fish | Every decision filtered through buyer's perspective | FIL-GB-004 |
| 4 | Test, don't guess | Never assume -- A/B test every major element | HEUR-GB-001 |
| 5 | Make the ad valuable | If the reader wouldn't clip and save the ad, rewrite it | FIL-GB-002 |
| 6 | Ethics filter | If the product can't deliver on the promise, don't write the ad | FIL-GB-008 |
| 7 | Stack proof types | Minimum 5 different proof types per piece | HEUR-GB-010 |
| 8 | Simplify first | Reduce to lemonade stand, then add complexity | MM-GB-005 |
